subject: Project Management
title: Waterfall in Education
description: A quick, practical guide to Waterfall in Education.

hook.text: Most computer science programs still teach waterfall first. Students learn it before they learn why it often fails.

buildup.text: Textbooks present waterfall as the foundational model, with agile introduced as an alternative later.

discovery.text: Teaching waterfall first makes pedagogical sense: it's simple, linear, and easy to diagram on a whiteboard.

twist.text: But graduates enter the workforce expecting waterfall and are shocked to find most teams use agile or hybrid.

climax.text: The gap between education and practice is slowly closing as universities add agile courses and projects.

punchline.text: We teach waterfall first because it's simple. Then reality teaches the rest.

quiz.question: Why do universities still teach waterfall first?
options: It's simple, linear, and easy to explain as a foundational model
It's the only method that works
Agile is too difficult to teach
